overview.title: Dice Audit Overview
overview.intro: This audit independently validates the Dice game operated by Duel.com across five domains: deterministic outcome generation, entropy integrity, live-to-verifier parity, RTP mathematical accuracy, and fairness integrity testing.
whatWasAudited: The RNG algorithm is deterministic and verifiable
Server seeds are cryptographically committed before play
Players can set their own client seeds
Nonces increment correctly and are never reused
Game outcomes determined by a provably fair algorithm
Players can independently verify every bet

rows.description: Server seed hashing, timing, reveal mechanics
Verifier vs live game result matching

guarantees: Live game results match the public verifier
No adversarial condition was able to violate any fairness guarantee at audit time

note.strong: All code, datasets, and simulation scripts are publicly accessible.
note.body: Anyone can independently verify every finding in this audit.

code.label: reproduce-audit.sh
lines: npm test    # 39 tests total — Dice: 13 tests (100% pass)

references.title: Dice — Game Rules
references.intro: Duel Dice is a number prediction game. Players set a target number and choose over/under. A provably fair roll between 0.00 and 99.99 determines the outcome. Payouts scale inversely with win probability — higher risk targets pay higher multipliers. House edge is fixed at 1% across all configurations.

sections.title: Game Rules
sections.content: Duel Dice is a number prediction game where players bet on whether a provably fair roll (0.00–99.99) will land over or under a chosen target number. Payouts scale inversely with win probability — higher risk targets pay higher multipliers. House edge is fixed at 1% across all configurations.

references.title: Why Provably Fair Matters
references.intro: Provably fair is a cryptographic system where game outcomes are determined by inputs from both the casino (server seed) and the player (client seed). The casino commits to its seed before the bet via a SHA-256 hash. After play, the seed is revealed and players can verify the outcome was predetermined.

sections.title: Why Provably Fair Matters
sections.content: Provably fair is a cryptographic system where game outcomes are determined by inputs from both the casino (server seed) and the player (client seed). The casino commits to its seed before the bet via a SHA-256 hash. After play, the seed is revealed and players can verify the outcome was predetermined. See provablyfair.org/learn for a detailed explainer.

sections.title: Seed, Nonce & Determinism
sections.subtitle: Can the casino change your outcome after you bet?
sections.intro: Every Dice roll on Duel is generated from three inputs: server seed, client seed, and nonce. The casino commits to its seed before you bet, you control your own seed, and the nonce increments automatically.

playerBrief.headline: Commit-Reveal Cryptographic Guarantee
verified: Casino commits to server seed before any bet
Players can set or change their client seed
Result derived only from (server seed, client seed, nonce)
Same inputs always produce same outcome
means: Casino cannot change outcomes after you bet
You contribute your own randomness
Every bet is unique and unpredictable
Any result can be verified independently
Results are tamper-proof, even months later

sectionVerdictTable.finding: Casino cannot change randomness after bet

verdict.title: Deterministic and Provably Fair
verdict.body: All Dice outcomes are fully deterministic and independently reproducible.

expandables.title: How It Works — Seed, Nonce & Determinism

subsections.title: Server Seed Commitment
subsections.content: Before any bet is placed, the casino generates a secret server seed and publicly commits to it by displaying its SHA-256 hash to the player. This cryptographic commitment prevents the casino from changing the seed after seeing player actions. When the player rotates their client seed, the previous server seed is revealed. The auditor verifies it by hashing it and confirming it matches the pre-committed hash.

subsections.title: Client Seed Control
subsections.content: Players have full control over their client seed through the Duel UI, allowing them to view, modify, or randomize it at any time before placing bets. The client seed is an input to every HMAC-SHA256 computation — changing it produces a different dice roll result.

subsections.title: Nonce Incrementation
subsections.content: The nonce begins at 0 and increments by 1 for each bet under the same seed pair, ensuring every bet produces a unique RNG input. The nonce resets to 0 only when the player rotates their client seed.

subsections.title: Deterministic Mapping
subsections.content: The RNG algorithm is fully deterministic: given the same server seed, client seed, and nonce, it always produces the exact same dice roll. This was verified across all 6,200 bets in the dataset — every single result matched the independently recomputed value.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section indexes the technical artifacts used to verify Dice seed handling, nonce behavior, and determinism. All evidence is reproducible using the linked scripts and datasets.

rows: Seed commit, client seed control, nonce sequencing, determinism tests
Core Dice RNG logic and range mapping

subsections.title: Datasets Used
subsections.content: Primary Dataset: duel-dice-sim-1767531771390.json — 6,200 live bets from duel.com/dice, schema duel-dice-sim-min-reveal-v2, 125 seed sessions. Fields used: serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, drawnNumber, result.

sections.title: RNG & Entropy Integrity
sections.subtitle: Is the randomness genuinely random, or could it be rigged?
sections.intro: This section verifies that Duel's random number generation produces cryptographically sound, unbiased outputs using HMAC-SHA256 with rejection sampling.

playerBrief.headline: Cryptographic Randomness Verification
verified: No hidden or external entropy sources
means: Rolls are genuinely random, not skewed
No number appears more often than expected
Casino cannot influence the randomness
As fair as a physical dice roll

verdict.title: Cryptographically Sound and Unbiased
verdict.body: RNG outputs are uniformly distributed with no detectable bias or manipulation surface.

subsections.title: RNG Function Implementation
subsections.content: The Duel Dice RNG implementation uses HMAC-SHA256 with deterministic inputs (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce) and employs rejection sampling against a calculated fair range (MAX_FAIR = 4,294,960,534) to eliminate modulo bias, producing unbiased dice outcomes from 0.00 to 100.00.

codeCard.label: src/dice/DiceNumbersGenerator.ts:3-33
lines: // Calculate the largest multiple of RANGE that fits in uint32
throw new Error("Failed to generate unbiased dice value from hash");

subsections.title: Entropy Sources
subsections.content: The system uses three cleanly separated entropy sources — server seed, client seed, and nonce — with no external contamination from timestamps, Math.random(), or server-side state.

subsections.title: Bias Elimination (Rejection Sampling)
subsections.content: Rejection sampling eliminates modulo bias by discarding any raw 32-bit values ≥ MAX_FAIR (4,294,960,534), ensuring the remaining values map uniformly to the 0–10,000 range with a rejection rate of only 0.000157%. Each outcome (0.00 to 100.00) has exactly equal probability.

subsections.title: RNG Isolation
subsections.content: The generateDiceResult() function is completely stateless — each bet's result depends solely on its unique (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce) input tuple. Different users receive different server seeds, and seed rotation ensures no state leaks between rounds.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section indexes the technical artifacts used to verify the Dice random number generator, its entropy sources, bias elimination, and isolation properties. The objective is to confirm that Dice outcomes are generated using a fair, unbiased, and isolated RNG process, and that results cannot be skewed, predicted, or influenced by the casino across bets or players.

rows: RNG dependency, entropy isolation, bias, and isolation tests
Core Dice RNG logic and range mapping

subsections.title: Datasets Used
subsections.content: Primary Dataset: duel-dice-sim-1767531771390.json — 6,200 live bets from duel.com/dice, schema duel-dice-sim-min-reveal-v2, 125 seed sessions. Fields used for RNG verification: serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, drawnNumber, result. No timestamps or external inputs were used in RNG computation.

subsections.title: RNG Dependency Verification
subsections.content: Dice outcomes depend only on (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce). Canonical example: serverSeed 808eaef57ae9f272ab01a1209b509948fb242fe1f14e135547bd10006e6196f3, clientSeed G3blCQBWQdVfM8sx, nonce 0 → observed result 25.28, recomputed result 25.28 ✅ MATCH. Full dataset: 6,200 bets verified, 6,200 matches, 0 mismatches, no external entropy detected.

subsections.title: Entropy Source Verification
subsections.content: No hidden or mixed entropy sources influence Dice outcomes. Verified sources: Server seed, Client seed, Nonce, HMAC-SHA256. Prohibited sources verified absent: Timestamps, Math.random(), External APIs, Server-side mutable state, Browser entropy.

subsections.title: Bias Elimination Verification
subsections.content: Every number between 0.00 and 100.00 has an equal chance. Mechanism: HMAC-SHA256 raw entropy → rejection sampling at MAX_FAIR → (value % 10001) / 100 mapping. Bias constants at DiceNumbersGenerator.ts:5-9, rejection loop at :17-27, bias test at DiceAuditExecutionChecklistTests.ts:74-76. No statistical skew or uneven distribution was observed.

subsections.title: RNG Isolation Verification
subsections.content: Outcomes cannot be predicted or manipulated across bets or players. Verified properties: Stateless RNG function, No cross-round memory, No cross-user contamination, No global or class state. Each bet is computed independently using a unique (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce) tuple.

rows: RNG depends only on declared inputs
No hidden or mixed entropy sources
RNG behavior is isolated per player

subsections.title: Reproduction Instructions
subsections.content: Clone the audit repository, install dependencies, then run: npm test -- --grep "Randomness & Entropy Model". Expected output: 4 tests passing — RNG depends only on (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce), No mixed entropy sources, Mapping from RNG → game ranges is unbiased, RNG state does not leak across rounds or users.

sections.title: Live ↔ Verifier Parity
sections.subtitle: Does what the casino shows you match what really happened?
sections.intro: This section tests whether live Dice game outcomes exactly match independent re-computation using the disclosed server seed, client seed, and nonce — confirming the verifier reflects real gameplay, not a simulation.

playerBrief.headline: Independent Recomputation of Every Bet
verified: No rounding errors or edge-case divergences
means: The result shown to you is the real result
Casino isn't displaying different outcomes
You can verify any bet yourself
Zero discrepancies across 6,200 bets

verdict.title: Perfect Parity — What You See Is What Happened
verdict.body: Every displayed result matches the independently recomputed outcome exactly.

subsections.title: What Parity Means
subsections.content: Parity verification tests whether the dice roll shown in the game UI matches what the auditor independently calculates using the same inputs. Both paths — live game and independent verifier — use identical HMAC-SHA256 logic. Any mismatch would indicate post-generation manipulation.

subsections.title: Methodology
subsections.content: For each of the 6,200 live bets, the auditor recomputed the expected dice roll from the disclosed serverSeed, clientSeed, and nonce using the DiceNumbersGenerator implementation. The result was compared against the drawnNumber field from the live dataset.

subsections.title: Results
subsections.content: 6,200 / 6,200 bets matched exactly. Zero discrepancies were found. The live game output is identical to the independently recomputed result for every recorded bet.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section documents the technical evidence for parity verification. All checks are reproducible using the audit repository and the provided dataset.

rows: Live parity tests and full dataset recomputation
Live game output matches independently recomputed result (6,200/6,200)
No rounding differences at any precision level

sections.title: RTP & Payout Validation
sections.subtitle: Does the house edge match what's advertised?
sections.intro: This section validates that Duel's Dice payout logic is mathematically correct and that the observed RTP converges to the advertised 99.9% across ~980,000 simulated rounds with no hidden payout distortion.

playerBrief.headline: Payout Fairness Verification
verified: Theoretical RTP matches payout table maths
No payout distortion or hidden edge
means: House edge is exactly what's advertised
No hidden advantage at any bet size
Long-term returns match expectations
Payout tables are mathematically correct

verdict.title: Payout Structure Mathematically Verified
verdict.body: RTP converges to theoretical expectations. No payout distortion identified.

subsections.title: Payout Formula
subsections.content: For Dice, the payout multiplier for any bet configuration is: multiplier = (100 / win_probability) × (1 - house_edge), where house_edge = 0.01 (1%). For example, rolling under 50 gives win_probability = 49.5/100 → multiplier ≈ 2.02×. The formula ensures RTP = 99.9% regardless of target number or over/under choice.

subsections.title: Monte Carlo Simulation
subsections.content: The auditor ran ~980,000 simulated dice rounds across representative target configurations. The observed RTP converged to 99.91%, within normal statistical variance of the 99.9% theoretical value. No configuration produced a systematically different edge.

subsections.title: Win Calculator Verification
subsections.content: The DiceWinCalculator implementation was tested against all expected multiplier values. All win amounts matched the formula-derived values within floating-point precision. No hidden adjustments or configuration-based payout changes were detected.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section documents the evidence for RTP and payout logic verification, covering the win calculator implementation, theoretical RTP derivation, and Monte Carlo simulation results.

rows: Payout verification for all multiplier configurations
Game profile and configuration completeness tests
Simulated RTP converges within expected variance
House edge consistent at 1% across all targets

sections.title: Fairness Integrity Testing
sections.subtitle: Can anyone cheat the system — players or the casino?
sections.intro: This section tests whether any party — player or casino — can predict outcomes, alter results, or gain an unfair advantage through weaknesses in the provably fair implementation. Testing covers 7 fairness guarantee categories under realistic gameplay conditions.

playerBrief.headline: Fairness Integrity Testing
means: No one — not the player, not the casino — can know or control the outcome before it is determined
Once a bet is placed, the result cannot be changed
Each bet is cryptographically unique and irreversible
Your results are independent of every other player's results

sectionVerdictTable.finding: Outcomes cannot be predicted before betting
Results cannot be altered after bet is placed
Each bet uses unique, sequential input
No hidden, mixed, or predictable entropy sources
Outcomes are fully isolated across rounds and users
Game parameters and payouts cannot be manipulated client-side

verdict.title: All Fairness Guarantees Verified
verdict.body: All provably fair integrity tests passed. No adversarial condition was able to violate any fairness guarantee under realistic gameplay conditions.

subsections.title: Framework Overview
subsections.content: Testing follows the ProvablyFair.org Fairness Integrity Framework — a structured methodology derived from real, historically observed failures in provably fair systems. Seven categories are tested independently: Outcome Prediction, Post-Bet Tamper Resistance, Seed Commitment Integrity, Nonce Uniqueness, Entropy Isolation, Round & Player Isolation, and Payout Integrity.

subsections.title: Outcome Prediction Test
subsections.content: Attempts to predict the dice roll before betting are impossible because the server seed is unknown to the player until after the seed is rotated. Without the server seed, the HMAC-SHA256 output cannot be precomputed. Result: Pass.

subsections.title: Post-Bet Tamper Resistance
subsections.content: Once a bet is placed, the nonce is consumed and the result is locked to the committed server seed. The server seed cannot be changed without breaking the SHA-256 commitment. Any change would be detected by any player verifying their bet. Result: Pass.

subsections.title: Nonce Uniqueness & Sequencing
subsections.content: Each nonce value is used exactly once per seed pair. Sequential nonce values ensure no two bets share the same HMAC input tuple. Nonce reuse would produce identical results for different bets, which would be detectable by independent verification. Result: Pass.

subsections.title: Round & Player Isolation
subsections.content: The Dice RNG function is completely stateless. Different players with different server seeds cannot influence each other's outcomes. Sequential bets from the same player under the same seed pair are fully isolated by the incrementing nonce. Result: Pass.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section documents the evidence used to verify all 7 fairness integrity guarantees. All checks are reproducible using the audit test suite.

rows: All fairness integrity tests including isolation, tamper resistance, and payout integrity
Outcome cannot be predicted before seed rotation
Server seed commitment is cryptographically binding
Nonce is unique per bet within a seed pair
RNG state does not leak across rounds or players
Payout logic is server-side and not client-manipulable

sections.title: Player Verification Guide
sections.subtitle: How any player can independently verify a game result
sections.intro: Every Dice outcome can be independently reproduced using publicly disclosed inputs. No hidden variables, no private backend data — if your calculated result matches the displayed result, the game was provably fair.

playerBrief.headline: Independent Result Verification
verified: Every Dice outcome can be independently reproduced
No hidden variables — no private backend data
If your calculated result matches the displayed result, the game was provably fair
Most players can verify directly through the Duel UI
means: Server Seed — found in the Verify tab (casino entropy)
Client Seed — found in the Verify tab (your entropy)
Nonce — found in the Verify tab (ensures uniqueness)
Result — from the Results tab (must match recomputed output)

walkthroughSteps.title: Place a Bet
walkthroughSteps.body: The game generates a result using the provably fair algorithm.

walkthroughSteps.title: Open the Verify Tab
walkthroughSteps.body: After the bet, seeds and nonce are disclosed to you.

walkthroughSteps.title: Recompute the Result
walkthroughSteps.body: Use the disclosed inputs with the verifier (or run the HMAC-SHA256 yourself) to recompute the outcome.

walkthroughSteps.title: Confirm the Match
walkthroughSteps.body: If the live result matches the recomputed result, the game was provably fair. If not, a mismatch is flagged.

sectionVerdictTable.check: Player can access all required inputs
sectionVerdictTable.finding: Server seed, client seed, nonce all exposed in Verify tab
Modal accessible from bet history on every bet
Standard crypto primitives only, no casino-specific black box

sections.verdictTitle: Any Player Can Reproduce Dice Results
sections.verdictBody: Only disclosed inputs are used. Identical inputs always produce identical output.

subsections.title: Step 1: Place a Bet
subsections.content: Set your target number and choose over or under. Place your bet. The game generates a result using the provably fair HMAC-SHA256 algorithm with your client seed, the casino's server seed, and the current nonce.

subsections.title: Step 2: Open the Verify Tab
subsections.content: After the bet resolves, click on the bet result in your bet history to open the details modal, then switch to the "Verify" tab. You'll see the server seed hash, client seed, and nonce.

subsections.title: Step 3: Recompute the Result
subsections.content: Use the disclosed server seed, client seed, and nonce with the verifier to recompute the dice roll. The formula: HMAC-SHA256(serverSeed, clientSeed:nonce) → extract first uint32 value < MAX_FAIR → (value % 10001) / 100.

subsections.title: Step 4: Confirm the Match
subsections.content: If the independently computed dice roll matches the result shown in the game, the bet was provably fair. If there is any mismatch, it indicates a possible integrity violation.

subsections.title: Verification Complete
subsections.content: You don't need to trust the casino's verifier. The inputs are standard cryptographic primitives (HMAC-SHA256) that you can run in any programming language.

note.body: You don't need to trust the casino's verifier. The inputs are standard cryptographic primitives (HMAC-SHA256) that you can run in any programming language.

subsections.title: Why Manual Verification Matters
subsections.content: True provably fair verification means you don't trust any casino-provided tool. Manual verification allows you to run calculations on your own machine, eliminate any possibility of a tampered verifier, and understand exactly how results are generated.

subsections.title: JavaScript Verification Script
subsections.content: Copy and run this in Node.js to verify any Dice bet:

codeCard.label: verify-dice.js
lines: throw new Error('Failed to generate unbiased value');
